methods.name: value_object
methods.docs: Creates the canonical empty-payload ValueObject role.

methods.name: entity
methods.docs: Creates a minimal Entity role with the conventional identity accessor.

methods.name: aggregate_root
methods.docs: Creates a minimal AggregateRoot role with the conventional identity accessor and empty optional vectors.

methods.name: domain_service
methods.docs: Creates the canonical empty-payload DomainService role.

methods.name: use_case
methods.docs: Creates the canonical empty-payload UseCase role.

methods.name: variant_name
methods.docs: Returns the payload-free role name used for display, parsing, and method reference signatures.

DataRole.docs: Role enum for TypeEntry (struct / enum / type alias). Declares the DDD / Clean Architecture role of a data type. Breaking change from baseline: data-carrying enum (Copy dropped); ValueObject / Entity / AggregateRoot / DomainService / UseCase gain per-variant semantics payload fields; DomainEvent (unit) and EventPolicy { reacts_to: NonEmptyVec<TypeRef> } added as new variants. Specification / Factory / Interactor / Command / Query / Dto / ErrorType / SecondaryAdapter remain unit variants.

methods.name: variant_name
methods.docs: Returns the payload-free role name used for display and parsing.

ContractRole.docs: Role enum for TraitEntry. Breaking change from baseline: data-carrying enum (Copy dropped); Repository { aggregate: TypeRef } variant added as an independent variant (aggregate field mandatory, Option not allowed — aggregate-less Repository is an illegal state). SpecificationPort / ApplicationService / SecondaryPort remain unit variants.

InvariantDecl.docs: Declares a named invariant for a domain type. Used in DataRole::ValueObject.invariants / Entity.invariants / AggregateRoot.invariants. `name` identifies the invariant (required, must not be empty); `predicate` names the verification mechanism (required). Both fields are required because a nameless invariant is unidentifiable and a predicate-free invariant is unverifiable.

InvariantName.docs: Validated name for a declared invariant. Wraps an Identifier (non-empty validated string). Used as the `name` field in InvariantDecl. A nameless invariant is unidentifiable, so the type enforces non-emptiness at construction.

InvariantPredicate.docs: The verification mechanism for an InvariantDecl. Currently a single-variant enum to preserve the extension axis for future predicate kinds (e.g., Specification predicate). SelfMethod(MethodName) designates a predicate method on the declaring type's self: the linter checks that the method exists in the type's public method surface with receiver=&self, no params, and bool return.

IdentityAccessor.docs: References the public getter method that exposes the identity of a DataRole::Entity or DataRole::AggregateRoot. Wraps a MethodName (validated method identifier). Public field identity is prohibited (Rust public fields are directly mutable from outside, undermining identity stability). The linter checks that the referenced method exists with &self receiver, no params, and a non-() return type.

ConstructionError.docs: Error type returned by `NonEmptyVec::try_new` when the input vector is empty.

NonEmptyVec.docs: A generic non-empty collection. Used as the element type for DataRole::EventPolicy.reacts_to (NonEmptyVec<TypeRef>), CatalogueLinterRuleKind::KindLayerConstraint.permitted_layers (NonEmptyVec<LayerId>), TraitImplRequired.required_traits (NonEmptyVec<String>), and NoRoleInMethodSignature.forbidden_roles (NonEmptyVec<RoleKind>). Enforces at the type level that the collection contains at least one element. No serde derives — domain layer is serde-free; the infrastructure codec converts JSON arrays to NonEmptyVec and treats an empty array as a decode error.

methods.name: from_data_role
methods.docs: Returns the payload-free discriminant for a DataRole.

methods.name: from_contract_role
methods.docs: Returns the payload-free discriminant for a ContractRole.

methods.name: variant_name
methods.docs: Returns a stable display name for this discriminant.

RoleKind.docs: Payload-free discriminant enum spanning all DataRole (15 variants) and ContractRole (4 variants) variants — 19 total. Used in RuleTarget.target_roles and in CatalogueLinterRuleKind payloads (e.g. NoRoleInMethodSignature.forbidden_roles) to specify roles without carrying payload data. DataRole is data-carrying after this track's breaking change, so a payload-bearing DataRole cannot be used directly as a rule selector; RoleKind provides a lightweight discriminant. FreeFunction and UseCaseFunction are FunctionRole variants, not represented here because linter rules target types/traits, not free functions.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a new RuleTarget from the given role list. An empty target_roles means all roles.

methods.name: all_roles
methods.docs: Creates a RuleTarget that matches all roles (empty target_roles).

methods.name: target_roles
methods.docs: Returns the target roles. An empty slice means all roles.

methods.name: matches
methods.docs: Returns true if the given RoleKind is in scope for this target. Always true when target_roles is empty (all_roles).

RuleTarget.docs: Selects which catalogue entries a CatalogueLinterRule applies to by role. An empty target_roles means the rule applies to all roles. Uses RoleKind (payload-free discriminant) so that Repository (a ContractRole variant) can be specified alongside DataRole variants in a single Vec.

methods.name: discriminant_name
methods.docs: Returns the discriminant name for display and violation reporting (e.g. 'FieldEmpty').

CatalogueLinterRuleKind.docs: Classifies the inspection content of a catalogue linter rule. 12-variant enum per ADR D15. Breaking change from baseline: all three existing variants gain payloads; 9 new variants added. All target_field/target_field payloads use String (not FieldName) — the domain linter works with raw strings to stay serde-free. KindLayerConstraint.permitted_layers is NonEmptyVec<LayerId>; TraitImplRequired.required_traits is NonEmptyVec<String>; NoRoleInMethodSignature.forbidden_roles is NonEmptyVec<RoleKind> — NonEmptyVec enforces non-emptiness at construction. MethodReferenceSignature carries only target_field: String (ADR D15 implementation-phase decision: receiver/params/returns constraints are evaluated by the linter body, not stored in the rule kind payload). ForbiddenMethodReceiver.forbidden_receiver is String (e.g. '&mut self') not SelfReceiver enum.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates and validates a new CatalogueLinterRule with the given target selector and inspection kind. Returns CatalogueLinterRuleError for empty validation inputs such as empty permitted_layers / required_traits / forbidden_roles / target_field.

methods.name: target
methods.docs: Returns the rule target selector.

methods.name: kind
methods.docs: Returns the inspection kind (what this rule checks).

CatalogueLinterRule.docs: A single catalogue linter rule. Breaking change from baseline: redesigned from a flat struct (rule_kind + target_kind: String + target_field + permitted_layers) to a 2-part structure where `target: RuleTarget` selects which roles the rule applies to and `kind: CatalogueLinterRuleKind` carries the inspection parameters. The old flat fields (target_kind: String, target_field, permitted_layers) move into the data-carrying CatalogueLinterRuleKind variants. All fields are private; access via accessor methods.

CatalogueLinterRuleError.docs: Errors returned by CatalogueLinterRule::new. Breaking change from baseline: EmptyTargetKind (removed — target_kind: String replaced by RuleTarget with Vec<RoleKind>) and the flat validation errors restructured. New variants: EmptyRequiredTraits (for TraitImplRequired with empty required_traits), EmptyForbiddenRoles (for NoRoleInMethodSignature with empty forbidden_roles), EmptyTargetField (for rule kinds requiring a target_field). EmptyPermittedLayers retained for KindLayerConstraint.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a new CatalogueLintViolation. rule_kind is the discriminant name from CatalogueLinterRuleKind::discriminant_name. No validation — violations are produced only by the trusted evaluator.

methods.name: rule_kind
methods.docs: Returns the discriminant name of the rule kind that generated this violation (e.g. 'FieldEmpty').

methods.name: entry_name
methods.docs: Returns the catalogue entry name that triggered the violation.

methods.name: message
methods.docs: Returns the human-readable violation message.

CatalogueLintViolation.docs: A single violation produced when a catalogue linter rule fires against an entry. All fields are private; access via accessor methods. rule_kind stores the &'static str discriminant name (from CatalogueLinterRuleKind::discriminant_name) rather than the CatalogueLinterRuleKind value itself — this avoids cloning data-carrying CatalogueLinterRuleKind in the violation. Modify from baseline: rule_kind type changes from old unit-variant enum reference to &'static str discriminant name string.

CatalogueLinterError.docs: Errors returned by the catalogue linter evaluation function. Modify from baseline: semantics shift from port error (impl Trait) to domain evaluation function error. The single variant InvalidRuleConfig(String) is retained.

CatalogueLoaderError.docs: Existing error type returned by CatalogueLoader implementations. Referenced unchanged because RunCatalogueLintInteractor retains the catalogue-loading port after the linter evaluation port is removed.

methods.name: load_all
methods.docs: Loads every TDDD-enabled layer catalogue for the given track.

CatalogueLoader.docs: Existing external-I/O port for loading per-layer catalogues. Referenced unchanged because D17 moves pure lint evaluation into domain while preserving ports for real external I/O.

CatalogueLinter.docs: Deleted in this track. The old CatalogueLinter secondary port exposed the run/evaluation path that D17 moves into the pure domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::evaluate_catalogue_lint function. No external-I/O linter operation is retained in the minimum scope of this track; callers supply rule lists directly, and future config/reporting I/O can be introduced as a separate port if needed.

domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::eval::ev.docs: Applies the given linter rules to the catalogue for the specified layer and returns all violations. Pure function with no external I/O — per D17, evaluation logic is placed in domain/application core. Returns the full list of violations found; an empty Vec means no rules fired. Errors if the rule configuration is internally inconsistent.

domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::preset::.docs: Returns the built-in ddd-strict preset: the complete minimum-core rule list covering D4-D11 / D16 / D18. The preset is assembled from CatalogueLinterRule values using RuleTarget and CatalogueLinterRuleKind.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a new InvariantDecl.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a new IdentityAccessor from an already-validated method name.

methods.name: method_name
methods.docs: Returns the referenced getter method name.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a new instance, validating the Rust identifier rules.

methods.name: as_str
methods.docs: Returns the underlying string slice.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Creates a NonEmptyVec with at least one element. Guarantees non-emptiness at construction.

methods.name: try_new
methods.docs: Creates a NonEmptyVec from a vector, rejecting empty input. Returns ConstructionError::EmptyCollection when `values` is empty.

methods.name: as_slice
methods.docs: Returns the elements as a slice.

methods.name: first
methods.docs: Returns the first element (always Some for a NonEmptyVec constructed via new/try_new, but the implementation delegates to Vec::first which returns Option).
